wikidata_candidates.label: Steve Fossett
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The name 'Stephen J. Fossett' on the card aligns with 'Stephen James (Steve) Fossett' in Wikidata. While the Wikidata entry emphasizes his later career as an adventurer and financier, biographical records for the famous Steve Fossett confirm that he worked as a systems engineer for IBM in the late 1960s (between his time at Stanford and his career in finance), which matches the card's employer and job title perfectly.

results.possibleIdentity: James Stephen "Steve" Fossett (1944–2007) was an American businessman and record-setting adventurer, primarily known for his global achievements in aviation, sailing, and ballooning. After receiving his MBA from the Olin School of Business at Washington University in St. Louis in 1968, Fossett began his professional career as a systems engineer for International Business Machines (IBM). Based in New York City during this period, he worked in the technical fields of computer systems and programming for approximately five years (c. 1968–1973). His background as a systems engineer at IBM led to his involvement with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that paired engineers and scientists with artists to foster creative collaborations. Fossett later transitioned into the financial services industry, becoming a successful commodities trader and a member of the New York Stock Exchange, before gaining worldwide fame for setting 115 world records as an adventurer.
